note: Covers birth through age 5. These milestones represent what 75% or more of children achieve by each age. Not a substitute for professional developmental screening.

Social_Emotional: Plays games such as peek-a-boo and pat-a-cake
Language_Communication: Waves bye-bye
Calls a parent mama or dada or another special name
Understands no — pauses briefly or stops when you say it
Cognition: Puts things in a container, like a block in a cup
Looks for things he sees you hide, like a toy under a blanket
Physical_Motor: Pulls to stand
Walks holding on to furniture
Drinks from a cup without a lid as you hold it
Picks things up between thumb and pointer finger

Social_Emotional: Moves away from you, but looks to make sure you are close by
Points to show you something interesting
Puts hands out for you to wash them
Looks at a few pages in a book with you
Helps you dress him by pushing arm through sleeve or lifting up foot
Language_Communication: Tries to say three or more words besides mama or dada
Follows one-step directions without gestures, like giving you a toy when you say give it to me
Cognition: Copies you doing chores, like sweeping with a broom
Plays with toys in a simple way, like pushing a toy car
Physical_Motor: Walks without holding on to anyone or anything
Scribbles
Drinks from a cup without a lid and may spill sometimes
Feeds herself with her fingers
Tries to eat with a spoon
Climbs on and off a couch or chair without help

Social_Emotional: Notices when others are hurt or upset, like pausing or looking sad when someone is crying
Looks at your face to see how to react in a new situation
Language_Communication: Points to things or pictures when you ask
Says at least two words together, like more milk
Points to at least two body parts when asked
Uses more gestures than just waving and pointing, like blowing a kiss or nodding yes
Cognition: Holds something in one hand while using the other hand
Tries to use switches, knobs, or buttons on a toy
Plays with more than one toy at the same time
Physical_Motor: Kicks a ball
Runs
Walks up a few stairs with or without help
Eats with a spoon

Social_Emotional: Plays next to other children and sometimes plays with them
Shows you what she can do by saying look at me
Follows simple routines when told, like helping to pick up toys at clean-up time
Language_Communication: Says about 50 words
Says two or more words together with one action word, like doggie run
Names things in a book when you point and ask what is this
Says words like I, me, or we
Cognition: Uses things to pretend, like feeding a block to a doll as if it were food
Shows simple problem-solving skills, like standing on a stool to reach something
Follows two-step instructions
Shows he knows at least one color
Physical_Motor: Uses hands to twist things, like turning doorknobs or unscrewing lids
Takes some clothes off by himself
Jumps off the ground with both feet
Turns book pages one at a time

Social_Emotional: Calms down within 10 minutes after you leave her at childcare or school
Notices other children and joins them to play
Language_Communication: Talks with you in conversation using at least two back-and-forth exchanges
Asks who, what, where, and why questions
Says what action is happening in a picture or book when asked
Says first name when asked
Talks well enough for others to understand most of the time
Cognition: Draws a circle when you show him how
Avoids touching hot objects like a stove when you warn her
Physical_Motor: Strings items together, like large beads or macaroni
Puts on some clothes by himself, like loose pants or a jacket
Uses a fork

Social_Emotional: Pretends to be something else during play such as teacher, superhero, dog
Asks to go play with children if none are around
Comforts others who are hurt or sad
Avoids danger, like not jumping from tall heights
Likes to be a helper
Changes behavior based on where she is
Language_Communication: Says sentences with four or more words
Says some words from a song, story, or nursery rhyme
Talks about at least one thing that happened during his day
Answers simple questions like what is a coat for
Cognition: Names a few colors of items
Tells what comes next in a well-known story
Draws a person with three or more body parts
Physical_Motor: Catches a large ball most of the time
Serves himself food or pours water with adult supervision
Unbuttons some buttons
Holds crayon or pencil between fingers and thumb not a fist

Social_Emotional: Follows rules or takes turns when playing games with other children
Sings, dances, or acts for you
Does simple chores at home, like matching socks or clearing the table
Language_Communication: Tells a story she heard or made up with at least two events
Answers questions about a book or story after you read or tell it to him
Keeps a conversation going with more than three back-and-forth exchanges
Uses or recognizes simple rhymes
Cognition: Counts to 10
Names some numbers between 1 and 5 when you point to them
Uses words about time, like yesterday, tomorrow, morning, or night
Pays attention for 5 to 10 minutes during activities
Writes some letters in her name
Names some letters when you point to them
Physical_Motor: Buttons some buttons
Hops on one foot
